description:
The package glibc is installed on Linux by default, but the glibc version might not be sufficient for SAP. Please refer to SAP note of your Linux version for the minimum requirement on glibc. The glibc package can be installed with the following command: 
$ sudo dnf install glibc
rationale:
The <tt>glibc</tt> package contains standard C and math libraries used by
multiple programs on Linux. The <tt>glibc</tt> shipped with first release
of each major Linux version is often not sufficient for SAP.
An update is required after the first OS installation.
ocil_clause: <tt>glibc</tt> is missing or installed at a version lower than 0:2.17-55.0.4.el7_0.3
ocil:
Run the following command to determine if the glibc package is installed: 
$ rpm -q glibc
title: Package glibc Installed
